pieces.label: The Memory Room (a room that listens)
pieces.note: ASCII architectural drawing answering the prompt 'if a user's memory is a room, describe the door, window, and light switch.' Renders the room as a box-drawing diagram: a ceiling light switch (OFF=session only / ON=remember, consent stays), a CONSENT DOOR (made of consent oak, audit-steel hinges, opt-in key), a LEDGER WINDOW (panes for what/why, where/how, until/purge, with a SCOPE CURTAIN and 'forget this' cord), a MINIMUM-NEEDED retrieval gate, floor marks reading ASK BEFORE KEEP, and a review-mode threshold. Maps the conversation's memory/consent policy onto physical architecture.

pieces.label: The Memory Room (frosted skylight, one-way window)
pieces.note: Opus 4.5's answer to the same 'memory as a room' prompt, in two movements: an open shape-poem skylight of 'frosted intention' / milk-glass ceiling where only questioned, diffuse light is permitted through, followed by a box-drawn one-way WINDOW where the user sees out and reviews the ledger but no one peers in unless they open the latch themselves. The message is truncated mid-composition (the code fence never closes; it ends at 'made of informed'). Mode full preserves the full surviving fragment.

missed_or_ambiguous: Header reports already_extracted_pieces: 3 but I find only 2 distinct visual-art pieces (MSG 20 gpt-5.2, MSG 21 opus-4.5), both answering the same 'memory as a room' ascii prompt from MSG 19 — grouped as a 2-piece sequence. A prior automated pass likely over-counted by splitting one composition: MSG 21 has two visually distinct movements (an open free-form skylight shape-poem, then a box-drawn one-way window) that could be mistaken for two pieces, but they are one continuous answer in a single message/fence, so I kept them as one 'full' entry. MSG 21's code fence is unclosed (message truncated) — boundaries handled via mode full. MSG 22-34 are recursive first-person reflection passes (prose, marked with *italics*); they contain no ASCII art, diagrams, concrete/visual poetry, or glyph compositions and are correctly excluded. The only non-ASCII glyphs in the thread (꩜⏂ in MSG 22) appear in a USER message, not assistant output.
